# Filing Templates — By Vehicle Type

## Template 1: Circuit Court Motion (14th Circuit)

```text
STATE OF MICHIGAN
IN THE CIRCUIT COURT FOR THE COUNTY OF MUSKEGON

ANDREW J. PIGORS,                    Case No. {{CASE_NUMBER}}
        Plaintiff/Petitioner,        Hon. {{JUDGE_NAME}}
    v.

EMILY A. WATSON,
        Defendant/Respondent.
_____________________________________/

                    {{MOTION_TITLE}}

    NOW COMES Plaintiff, ANDREW J. PIGORS, appearing pro se, and
respectfully moves this Honorable Court for an Order {{RELIEF_SUMMARY}},
and in support thereof states as follows:

STATEMENT OF FACTS

    1. {{FACT_1}} (Exhibit {{EX_NUM}}, p. {{PAGE}}.)

    2. {{FACT_2}}

    3. {{FACT_3}}

LEGAL ARGUMENT

I. {{ISSUE_HEADING_1}}

    A. Issue

    {{ISSUE_STATEMENT}}

    B. Rule

    Under {{AUTHORITY}}, "{{QUOTED_RULE_TEXT}}." {{CITE}}.

    C. Application

    In this case, {{APPLICATION_TO_FACTS}}. The evidence shows {{EVIDENCE_REF}}.

    D. Conclusion

    Therefore, {{CONCLUSION_1}}.

II. {{ISSUE_HEADING_2}}

    [Repeat IRAC structure]

RELIEF REQUESTED

    WHEREFORE, Plaintiff respectfully requests that this Court:

    a. {{RELIEF_ITEM_1}};

    b. {{RELIEF_ITEM_2}};

    c. Grant such other and further relief as this Court deems just and
       equitable.

VERIFICATION

    I, Andrew J. Pigors, declare under the penalties of perjury that the
facts stated in this motion are true to the best of my information,
knowledge, and belief.

Respectfully submitted,

Date: {{DATE}}             _____________________________
                           Andrew J. Pigors, Pro Se
                           {{ADDRESS_LINE_1}}
                           {{ADDRESS_LINE_2}}
                           {{PHONE}}
                           {{EMAIL}}
```

## Template 2: Brief in Support (MCR 2.119(A)(2))

```text
STATE OF MICHIGAN
IN THE CIRCUIT COURT FOR THE COUNTY OF MUSKEGON

[Caption block — same as motion]

                BRIEF IN SUPPORT OF {{MOTION_TITLE}}

INDEX OF AUTHORITIES

Cases                                                     Page(s)

{{CASE_1}}, {{CITE_1}} .................................... {{PAGE}}
{{CASE_2}}, {{CITE_2}} .................................... {{PAGE}}

Statutes

{{MCL_1}} ................................................. {{PAGE}}
{{MCL_2}} ................................................. {{PAGE}}

Court Rules

{{MCR_1}} ................................................. {{PAGE}}
{{MCR_2}} ................................................. {{PAGE}}

QUESTIONS PRESENTED

    I. Whether {{QUESTION_1}}?

    Plaintiff answers: Yes.

    II. Whether {{QUESTION_2}}?

    Plaintiff answers: Yes.

STATEMENT OF FACTS

    {{NARRATIVE_FACTS_WITH_RECORD_CITATIONS}}

ARGUMENT

I. {{ARGUMENT_HEADING_1}}

    A. Standard of Review

    {{STANDARD_OF_REVIEW}} {{CITE}}.

    B. Issue

    {{ISSUE_STATEMENT}}

    C. Rule

    {{RULE_WITH_PINPOINT_CITE}}

    D. Application

    {{APPLICATION_TO_CASE_FACTS}}

    E. Conclusion

    {{SECTION_CONCLUSION}}

II. {{ARGUMENT_HEADING_2}}

    [Repeat IRAC structure]

CONCLUSION AND RELIEF REQUESTED

    For the foregoing reasons, Plaintiff respectfully requests that this
Court {{SPECIFIC_RELIEF}}.

                           Respectfully submitted,

Date: {{DATE}}             _____________________________
                           Andrew J. Pigors, Pro Se
```

## Template 3: Proposed Order (MCR 2.602)

```text
STATE OF MICHIGAN
IN THE CIRCUIT COURT FOR THE COUNTY OF MUSKEGON

[Caption block]

          ORDER {{GRANTING/DENYING}} {{MOTION_TYPE}}

    At a session of said Court, held in the City of Muskegon,
County of Muskegon, State of Michigan, on _____________, 20___.

    PRESENT: HONORABLE {{JUDGE_NAME}}, Circuit Court Judge.

    This matter having come before the Court on Plaintiff's
{{MOTION_TYPE}}, and the Court having reviewed the motion, brief,
and supporting exhibits, and being otherwise fully advised in the
premises;

    IT IS HEREBY ORDERED that:

    1. {{ORDER_PROVISION_1}}.

    2. {{ORDER_PROVISION_2}}.

    3. {{ORDER_PROVISION_3}}.

    IT IS SO ORDERED.


                           _____________________________
                           Hon. {{JUDGE_NAME}}
                           Circuit Court Judge

Date: _______________
```

## Template 4: Certificate of Service (MCR 2.107)

```text
                    CERTIFICATE OF SERVICE

    I hereby certify that on {{SERVICE_DATE}}, I served a true and
correct copy of the foregoing {{DOCUMENT_LIST}} upon the following
by the method indicated:

{{PARTY_NAME}}                        [ ] First-class U.S. Mail
{{PARTY_ADDRESS_1}}                   [ ] Personal delivery
{{PARTY_ADDRESS_2}}                   [ ] MiFILE e-filing service
{{PARTY_CITY_STATE_ZIP}}              [ ] Email: {{EMAIL}}

{{FOC_NAME}}                          [ ] First-class U.S. Mail
{{FOC_ADDRESS}}                       [ ] Personal delivery
{{FOC_CITY_STATE_ZIP}}                [ ] MiFILE e-filing service


                           _____________________________
                           Andrew J. Pigors, Pro Se
                           {{ADDRESS}}
                           {{CITY_STATE_ZIP}}
                           {{PHONE}}
Date: {{DATE}}
```

## Template 5: COA Appeal Brief (MCR 7.212)

```text
STATE OF MICHIGAN
IN THE COURT OF APPEALS

ANDREW J. PIGORS,                    Court of Appeals No. {{COA_NUMBER}}

        Plaintiff-Appellant,         Circuit Court No. {{CC_NUMBER}}
    v.

EMILY A. WATSON,

        Defendant-Appellee.
_____________________________________/

               APPELLANT'S BRIEF ON APPEAL

ORAL ARGUMENT REQUESTED / NOT REQUESTED

TABLE OF CONTENTS
                                                          Page
Index of Authorities ....................................... ii
Statement of Jurisdiction .................................. 1
Statement of Questions Presented ........................... 2
Statement of Facts ......................................... 3
Arguments .................................................. {{PAGE}}
    I.  {{ISSUE_1_HEADING}} ................................ {{PAGE}}
    II. {{ISSUE_2_HEADING}} ................................ {{PAGE}}
Conclusion and Relief Requested ............................ {{PAGE}}
Appendix ................................................... {{PAGE}}

INDEX OF AUTHORITIES

Cases                                                     Page(s)

{{CASE_CITATIONS_WITH_PAGES}}

Statutes

{{STATUTE_CITATIONS_WITH_PAGES}}

Court Rules

{{RULE_CITATIONS_WITH_PAGES}}

STATEMENT OF JURISDICTION

    {{JURISDICTION_BASIS — MCR 7.203 or 7.205}}

STATEMENT OF QUESTIONS PRESENTED

    I. {{QUESTION_1}}

       Appellant answers: {{YES/NO}}.
       Trial Court answered: {{YES/NO}}.

STATEMENT OF FACTS

    {{CHRONOLOGICAL_FACTS_WITH_RECORD_CITATIONS}}

    (Lower Court Register of Actions: Appendix, pp. {{PAGES}}.)

ARGUMENT

I. {{ISSUE_HEADING}}

    A. Standard of Review

    {{STANDARD}} See {{CASE_CITE}}.

    B. The trial court {{erred/abused its discretion}} when it {{ACTION}}.

    [IRAC structure with record citations]

    C. Alternatively, this constitutes plain error under People v Carines,
       460 Mich 750, 763; 597 NW2d 130 (1999).

    [Plain error analysis if issue not preserved]

CONCLUSION AND RELIEF REQUESTED

    For the reasons stated above, Appellant respectfully requests that
this Honorable Court {{REVERSE/REMAND/VACATE}} the trial court's
{{ORDER/JUDGMENT}} dated {{DATE}}.

                           Respectfully submitted,

Date: {{DATE}}             _____________________________
                           Andrew J. Pigors
                           Plaintiff-Appellant, Pro Se
```

## Template 6: MSC Emergency Application

```text
STATE OF MICHIGAN
IN THE SUPREME COURT

ANDREW J. PIGORS,                    Supreme Court No. ___________

        Plaintiff-Appellant,         Court of Appeals No. {{COA_NO}}
    v.                               Circuit Court No. {{CC_NO}}

EMILY A. WATSON,

        Defendant-Appellee.
_____________________________________/

      EMERGENCY APPLICATION FOR LEAVE TO APPEAL

    Plaintiff-Appellant, Andrew J. Pigors, appearing pro se,
respectfully files this Emergency Application for Leave to Appeal
pursuant to MCR 7.305 and MCR 7.313(A), and states:

I. STATEMENT OF QUESTIONS

    {{QUESTIONS_OF_STATEWIDE_SIGNIFICANCE}}

II. STATEMENT OF FACTS

    {{FACTS_WITH_RECORD_CITATIONS}}

III. ARGUMENT FOR WHY LEAVE SHOULD BE GRANTED

    A. The Court of Appeals decision conflicts with a Supreme Court
       decision.

    B. The questions involved are of significant public interest.

    C. Immediate review is necessary to prevent irreparable harm.

    {{IRAC_ARGUMENTS}}

IV. EMERGENCY RELIEF REQUESTED

    {{SPECIFIC_RELIEF — stay, immediate hearing, etc.}}

    Attached: Lower court orders, COA decision, proof of service.

    (13 copies filed per MCR 7.305(A).)
```

## Template 7: JTC Formal Complaint

```text
STATE OF MICHIGAN
JUDICIAL TENURE COMMISSION

In the Matter of:                    File No. ___________

HON. {{JUDGE_NAME}},
    {{COURT_NAME}} Judge.
_____________________________________/

              FORMAL COMPLAINT

    Complainant, Andrew J. Pigors, respectfully submits this Formal
Complaint against the Honorable {{JUDGE_NAME}} and states:

RESPONDENT

    1. Respondent is a judge of the {{COURT_NAME}}, having been
{{elected/appointed}} in {{YEAR}}, currently serving in {{DIVISION}}.

FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS

    2. {{SPECIFIC_ACTS_WITH_DATES_AND_CASE_NUMBERS}}

COUNT I — VIOLATION OF CANON {{NUMBER}}

    3. {{CANON_TEXT_QUOTED}}

    4. Respondent violated this Canon by {{SPECIFIC_CONDUCT}}.

    5. Evidence: {{EXHIBIT_REFERENCES}}

COUNT II — VIOLATION OF CANON {{NUMBER}}

    [Repeat structure]

PATTERN OF MISCONDUCT

    6. The foregoing acts constitute a pattern of {{TYPE}} misconduct
spanning {{TIME_PERIOD}}, across {{NUMBER}} proceedings.

PRAYER FOR RELIEF

    WHEREFORE, Complainant respectfully requests that the Commission:

    a. Investigate the allegations herein;
    b. Issue a public censure / recommend removal;
    c. Take such other action as deemed appropriate.

VERIFICATION

    I, Andrew J. Pigors, state under oath that the facts stated in
this Complaint are true to the best of my knowledge.

Date: {{DATE}}             _____________________________
                           Andrew J. Pigors
                           {{ADDRESS}}
```

## Template 8: Federal §1983 Complaint

```text
UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT
WESTERN DISTRICT OF MICHIGAN
SOUTHERN DIVISION

ANDREW J. PIGORS,                    Case No. _______________

        Plaintiff,                   Hon. _______________
    v.

{{DEFENDANT_1}}, individually and
in {{his/her}} official capacity;
{{DEFENDANT_2}}, individually and
in {{his/her}} official capacity;
[additional defendants]

        Defendants.
_____________________________________/

        COMPLAINT UNDER 42 U.S.C. § 1983

JURISDICTION AND VENUE

    1. This Court has subject-matter jurisdiction under 28 U.S.C.
§ 1331 (federal question) and 28 U.S.C. § 1343 (civil rights).

    2. Venue is proper under 28 U.S.C. § 1391(b) because the events
giving rise to Plaintiff's claims occurred in this district.

PARTIES

    3. Plaintiff Andrew J. Pigors is a resident of Muskegon County,
Michigan.

    4. Defendant {{NAME}} is {{TITLE/POSITION}} who at all relevant
times acted under color of state law.

FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS

    5. {{CHRONOLOGICAL_FACTS}}

COUNT I — DEPRIVATION OF DUE PROCESS (14th Amendment)

    6. {{FACTUAL_INCORPORATION}}

    7. Defendants deprived Plaintiff of his constitutionally protected
liberty interest in the care, custody, and control of his child
without due process of law, in violation of the Fourteenth Amendment.

    8. {{SPECIFIC_DUE_PROCESS_VIOLATIONS}}

COUNT II — EQUAL PROTECTION (14th Amendment)

    [Structure repeats]

COUNT III — FIRST AMENDMENT RETALIATION

    [Structure repeats]

QUALIFIED IMMUNITY IS INAPPLICABLE

    {{N}}. The rights at issue were clearly established at the time of
Defendants' conduct. See {{BINDING_PRECEDENT}}.

PRAYER FOR RELIEF

    WHEREFORE, Plaintiff requests:

    a. Compensatory damages in excess of ${{AMOUNT}};
    b. Punitive damages against each Defendant individually;
    c. Declaratory judgment that Defendants violated Plaintiff's
       constitutional rights;
    d. Injunctive relief {{SPECIFIC}};
    e. Costs and reasonable attorney's fees under 42 U.S.C. § 1988;
    f. Such other relief as the Court deems just.

JURY DEMAND

    Plaintiff demands a trial by jury on all issues so triable.

                           Respectfully submitted,

Date: {{DATE}}             _____________________________
                           Andrew J. Pigors, Pro Se
                           {{ADDRESS}}
                           {{PHONE}}
                           {{EMAIL}}
```

## Placeholder Reference

| Placeholder | Source | Example |
|-------------|--------|---------|
| `{{CASE_NUMBER}}` | Case matrix | 2024-001507-DC |
| `{{JUDGE_NAME}}` | Case matrix | Jenny L. McNeill |
| `{{COA_NUMBER}}` | Case matrix | 366810 |
| `{{DATE}}` | Runtime | March 5, 2026 |
| `{{MOTION_TITLE}}` | Vehicle name | EMERGENCY MOTION TO RESTORE PARENTING TIME |
| `{{AUTHORITY}}` | `auth_rules` query | MCR 3.210(C)(2) |
| `{{EVIDENCE_REF}}` | `evidence_quotes` query | (Exhibit A, p. 3) |
| `{{FACT_N}}` | `evidence_quotes` + provenance | Verbatim from DB |
| `{{EMAIL}}` | User fills at filing time | [EMAIL] placeholder |
| `{{ADDRESS}}` | User fills at filing time | [ADDRESS] placeholder |
